Kuakini Geriatric Care, Inc.

Honolulu, Honolulu County, Hawaii

  • Skilled Nursing

Contact & Location

Address
347 North Kuakini Street, Honolulu, HI 96817 · Map
Phone
(808) 547-9357
Capacity
187 residents
Community size
Large community

About Kuakini Geriatric Care, Inc.

Kuakini Geriatric Care, Inc. is a large, nonprofit, Medicare- and Medicaid-certified skilled nursing facility in the Kuakini Medical Center area of Honolulu. It is licensed for 187 beds and provides long-term skilled nursing care. Recent federal Care Compare data show an overall rating well below the state average.

Care offered here

  • Skilled Nursing — what skilled nursing includes and who it fits

Care needs usually grow over time. Ask every community what happens when your parent needs more care than their current level — moving twice is hard on everyone. See levels of care explained.

Lifestyle & Services

Every community sets its own rules on pets, couples moving in together, and age requirements — and its own menu of medical and non-medical services, including transportation. Ask for the current policies and a full service list in writing; details here can change and anything unlisted should be confirmed on your tour.

Rating at a glance

Below our recommended bar

This is below the bar we generally recommend. We still list it, with its real rating, because it may be the best or only nearby option for some families — see what the rating actually reflects below.

★★★★★

2 of 5 — Medicare rating
A government score, from health inspections and staffing — not customer reviews.

Do you work at Kuakini Geriatric Care, Inc.?

Families make one of their biggest decisions with pages like this one — and nobody can describe your community better than the people who work there. If you're on staff or manage this community, you can update these details, answer short topic-by-topic questionnaires (each takes just a few minutes), and share photos of your community. It's free, and accurate listings help the right families find you.

Claim & update this listing

Ratings & official records

Here is every rating we found for Kuakini Geriatric Care, Inc. — who assigns each one and what it actually measures — so you can weigh it for what it's worth. No single number decides a good fit; use these to build questions, then trust your own visit.

★★★★★ 2 of 5

Medicare Five-Star Quality Rating

Who assigns it: the federal Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), the government agency that certifies nursing homes. What it measures: independent state health inspections, nurse and aide staffing levels, and clinical quality measures, combined into one overall star. Some of the quality data is reported by the facility itself — which means a community that documents every incident honestly can score lower than one that reports less completely. Read the health-inspection detail, not just the summary star.

See the full federal report on Medicare Care Compare · How these stars are calculated, and their limits

Family
reviews

Family & resident reviews

Who writes it: families and residents, on public review sites. What it reflects: day-to-day life — food, friendliness, communication — not inspections. Read the recent ones and the middle-star ones, not just the average.

Search the community's name on Google or a senior-living review site to read the actual reviews — we only show the aggregate rating here.

A star average is a starting point, not a verdict. We don't write these reviews, verify what they say, or investigate the community ourselves — we aggregate public ratings and point you to where they live so you can do your own research. Before you weigh a number, know what it can hide:

  • Who writes them. Usually adult children, not the resident — so reviews capture communication, billing, and first impressions more than the texture of daily care. More →
  • Emotion moves the score. People post when they're delighted or furious; the quietly satisfied middle rarely does, and reviews written in grief, guilt, or relief carry that charge. The calm three-star reviews usually hold the real trade-offs. More →
  • Reviews can be solicited or self-reported. A community can fairly ask happy families to post — or cross the line by gating out unhappy ones, pressuring residents, or paying. A burst of five-star reviews in a single week is a flag, not a triumph. More →
  • Counts and dates matter. A 5.0 from three reviews is a coin flip, and new management makes a 2019 review irrelevant. Small homes gather few reviews by nature — lean on the state record and your own visit. Small homes →
  • Government stars are a different thing. Medicare's nursing-home stars aren't reviews at all — they come from inspections, staffing, and clinical data. How those work →

Read the full guide to reading reviews without being misled →

Below our recommended bar — here's why it's still here. A lower rating doesn't automatically mean no family should choose it, especially if it's the closest or only realistic option, and frequent visits are possible — presence and attention from family can meaningfully improve a resident's day-to-day care. See our note on weighing distance against rating. Read the record above closely, ask the community directly about what you find, and compare it against nearby alternatives before deciding. We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice — for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved.
